title: COP28 Health Day: A Watershed Moment for Climate and Health
summary: Climate change is not just an environmental issue; it's a health crisis touching every corner of our cities and stretching to the farthest reaches of our planet. COP28 has acknowledged this by introducing the inaugural 'Health Day', integrating a crucial health perspective into the global climate dialogue. The groundbreaking 'COP28 UAE Declaration on Climate and Health', endorsed by 124 countries, highlights the urgent need to fortify healthcare systems against climate-induced health challenges, ranging from infectious diseases and air pollution to extreme heat.
body: Climate change is not just an environmental issue; it's a health crisis touching every corner of our cities and stretching to the farthest reaches of our planet. COP28 has acknowledged this by introducing the inaugural 'Health Day', integrating a crucial health perspective into the global climate dialogue. The groundbreaking 'COP28 UAE Declaration on Climate and Health', endorsed by 124 countries, highlights the urgent need to fortify healthcare systems against climate-induced health challenges, ranging from infectious diseases and air pollution to extreme heat. Voices from COP28 on Climate-Health Nexus 1. Shweta Narayan (International Climate and Health Campaigner, Health Care Without Harm): “The essence of the Hippocratic oath, to first do no harm, demands our immediate action against the climate crisis.” 2. John Kerry (United States Special Presidential Envoy for Climate): “Violating our Hippocratic oath, we cause harm only to seek cures from the very doctors we burden. This cycle is sheer insanity.” Health and Climate: An Intertwined Reality 3. Mia Mottley (Prime Minister of Barbados): “The harsh realities of wildfires, food and water scarcity, and heatwaves underscore the need to proactively address potential health crises, including emerging pathogens.” 4. Jane Burston (CEO, Clean Air Fund): “Recognizing the health impacts of climate negotiations marks a pivotal shift. It’s not just about cutting emissions; it's about ending health issues like asthma and improving our quality of life.” Historic Health Day 5. Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus (Director, World Health Organization): “Health can be a catalyst for climate action, as it is a universal concern.” 6. Mafalda Duarte (Executive Director, Green Climate Fund): “Leverage health as a tool for climate advocacy. We’ve seen the costs of unprepared health systems during the pandemic, and climate change poses even greater challenges.” Looking Forward 7. Omnia El Omrani (Youth Envoy COP28 and Medical Doctor, Egypt): “There's a desire for health-centered climate solutions, especially among young healthcare professionals ready to drive change.” 8. Her Excellency Reem Ebrahim Al Hashimy (UAE Minister of State for International Cooperation): “Health is the personal face of climate change, making it our collective responsibility to protect our climate for the health and well-being of present and future generations.” Call to Action: Amplifying Voices for Change The commitments made during COP28's Health Day signal a transformative moment in the climate and global health agenda. But this is just the beginning. To build resilient health systems and prevent future climate-related health crises, we need more voices, more action. If you're a healthcare professional, join the WHO's call to action. Advocate for better health for all, and be part of this crucial change. Your voice matters in shaping a healthier, more sustainable future. Here’s What It Means for Severity, Vaccines, and Treatment Author Saad Amer

_list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article discusses the significance of COP28's inaugural Health Day, marking a pivotal integration of health perspectives into global climate policy. It highlights the 'COP28 UAE Declaration on Climate and Health', endorsed by 124 nations, which emphasizes strengthening healthcare systems against climate-induced health threats like infectious diseases, air pollution, and extreme heat. Key voices from the event stress the urgency: health professionals cite the Hippocratic oath to demand climate action, while leaders note the intertwined crises of wildfires, food scarcity, and pathogens. The Health Day is framed as a catalyst, using universal health concerns to drive climate advocacy and resilience-building, especially after pandemic-era strains on health systems. The piece calls for continued advocacy and action from healthcare professionals to shape sustainable futures.
result.main_content_zh: 本文探讨了COP28首次设立“健康日”的重要意义，标志着健康视角被关键性地纳入全球气候政策。文章重点介绍了由124个国家签署的《COP28阿联酋气候与健康宣言》，该宣言强调加强医疗系统以应对气候变化引发的健康威胁，如传染病、空气污染和极端高温。与会关键人士强调了紧迫性：卫生专业人员援引希波克拉底誓言要求采取气候行动，而领导人则指出野火、粮食短缺和病原体等交织的危机。健康日被视为一个催化剂，利用普遍的健康关切来推动气候倡导和韧性建设，尤其是在大流行时期卫生系统承压之后。文章呼吁卫生专业人员持续倡导和采取行动，以塑造可持续的未来。
